scope: add --signals alias for --select in drift scan (CLI contract parity)
add validate --baseline with progress comparison (before/after score delta, resolved/new counts)
add noise_context field in drift diff responses (pre_existing_count + explanation)
add path_diagnostic for empty target_path scopes in fix-plan
add skipped_files and skipped_languages to drift scan response
fix duplicate entries in fix_first (api._fix_first_concise + json_output._fix_first_list)
fix duplicate paths in related_files in agent_tasks (dict.fromkeys order-preserving dedup)
fix stale 'drift analyze' CLI references in success_criteria -> 'drift scan'
improve top_signals tie-breaking: use finding_count as secondary sort key
improve diff next_actions: hint baseline workflow when no baseline and noise present
notes: Agent-workflow analysis from real-world Coding Agent testing (drift_agent_test_2026-03-30.md) revealed 6 categories of deficiencies: duplicate fix_first entries, confusing drift diff output on dirty worktrees, misleading drift validate, CLI contract deviation (--signals vs --select), silent empty scope results, and hidden skipped file counts.

All 10 improvements are backward-compatible. New fields (noise_context, path_diagnostic, skipped_files) are additive to existing response structures. The --signals alias maps to the same parameter as --select.

Empirical validation: 4 new targeted tests verify CLI contract parity (--signals, --baseline option), noise-context hint presence, and path_diagnostic for empty scopes. Full test suite remains green at 1065 tests (net +4 targeted tests).
